description:
keyring-3.6.3/src/keyutils.rs:147-152 rejects empty secrets:

```rust
if secret.is_empty() {
    return Err(ErrorCode::Invalid("secret".to_string(), "cannot be empty".to_string()));
}
```

This becomes `keyring::Error::Invalid` and falls through to the catch-all arm at secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs:169-172 which maps it to `SecretError::Backend { backend: "keyring", source: <boxed keyring::Error::Invalid> }`.

Three problems:
1. The doc on `KeyringBackend::put` (lib.rs:51) says only 'value must be valid UTF-8'. It does not warn that the empty string is rejected. A user storing an empty value gets a non-obvious `Backend` error instead of a clear precondition violation.
2. There is no test exercising the empty-value rejection. The integration test at lib.rs:286-335 tests only `b"test-secret-value"`. A regression where empty values silently succeed (or cause a different error variant) would not be caught.
3. `SecretError::DecodeFailed` is the natural fit for 'value violates backend precondition', but the current code uses `DecodeFailed` only for non-UTF-8, not for empty. A consistent error model for caller-controllable preconditions would help.

Trigger: caller writes `store.put(SecretValue::new(Vec::new())).await` expecting a clear error and gets a generic Backend variant with the keyring crate's internal phrasing leaking through Display.

Resolution: (a) document the empty-value precondition on `put`; (b) add a unit test that does not require kernel keyring access — the precondition can be checked before spawn_blocking (mirroring the UTF-8 check at lib.rs:151-155 which IS already pre-flight).
title: Empty-secret rejection by underlying keyutils is undocumented and untested
